metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 — specify the exact `rebar` CLI invocation (e.g., `rebar identity lookup --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) that returns the identity ticket ID, confirming email-to-identity resolution in the rebar system, not just a local git config read.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Git-email resolution maps to this identity') has no concrete proving command in the plan body or ACs. The body mentions 'git config user.email' but that only reads a local config setting; it does not demonstrate that the rebar identity system resolves that email address to the identity ticket. No rebar CLI command (e.g., a lookup or resolve subcommand) is specified to produce evidence of the mapping.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; plan body bullet 'Verify TOFU registration'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the body bullet with: 'Verify email resolution: `rebar identity lookup --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` returns the bot identity ticket.' Update AC2 to: 'Running `rebar identity lookup --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` returns the Rebar Bot identity ticket.' If no such subcommand exists, document the alternative (e.g., `rebar list --type identity | grep joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) and explain what output constitutes proof.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the key-handling procedure for the bootstrap window: specify where keygen runs (e.g., in-memory only or encrypted volume), how the key is transmitted to SSM/GH Actions (e.g., direct `aws ssm put-parameter --type SecureString` pipe without touching disk), and that local plaintext copies are destroyed immediately after upload.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare a key-handling procedure for the ephemeral window between private-key generation on a developer workstation and upload to the secrets manager. During that window the key exists in plaintext on a local machine with no stated controls (e.g., generated in-memory only, immediately piped to SSM CLI, workstation disk encryption assumed, key material deleted after upload).
findings.location: Plan body — 'Generate an ed25519 keypair for the bot (private key never committed)' and AC: 'Private key generated and its secure-storage destinations recorded; nothing secret committed.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan step: 'Generate the key with `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /dev/stdout` (no disk write), pipe or directly pass the private key to `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key --type SecureString --value "$(...)"` in a single shell command; confirm no key material is retained in shell history (`HISTFILE=/dev/null`) or temp files.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step (or note to per-env tasks) that CI environments must configure `git config user.email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (or use `rebar identity use <id>` with a persisted pointer) so that `resolve_current_identity()` can resolve the bot identity in stateless runners.
findings.finding: The AC and body treat 'git config user.email resolves to this identity' as a verification step, but in stateless CI runners (GitHub Actions, AWS) there is no persisted `current_identity` pointer file (it is local/git-ignored per the codebase). The email-based fallback in `resolve_current_identity()` therefore only works if `git config user.email` is explicitly set to `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` in those environments. The plan does not specify this as a required configuration step, leaving a hidden precondition gap for the CI use cases.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Verify TOFU registration and that a git config user.email = joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com resolves to this identity'; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: 'Per-env tasks must configure git to use the bot email (`git config user.email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) OR write a persisted `current_identity` pointer, since the pointer file is git-ignored and absent in fresh CI runners; without this, `resolve_current_identity()` returns None and events are unattributed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the 'nothing secret committed' claim in AC3 — for example, `git log --all --full-history -- '*.pem' '*.key' '*signing*key*'` returns no results, or reference a secret-scanning CI check that covers the commit range.
findings.finding: AC3 includes 'nothing secret committed' as a claim but provides no verification command to confirm the private key was not accidentally committed to the repository.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Verified by: `git log --all -- id_ed25519 id_ed25519.pub` returns no results (or equivalent secret-scan CI gate passes on the PR).'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (in the plan or runbook) the expected behavior of `rebar identity create` on a re-run (idempotent / error / duplicate) and add a recovery step for partial-failure scenarios.
findings.finding: The plan does not address idempotency of `rebar identity create`: if the operator runs the command a second time (e.g., after a partial failure or accidental re-run), the outcome (error, silent duplicate, overwrite) is unspecified and the recovery path is undocumented.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Create the identity: rebar identity create …' step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a verification step before creation: 'if `rebar list --type identity` already shows Rebar Bot, skip creation and verify the public key matches; otherwise create.' Document the rollback procedure if the key is registered but the ticket is malformed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `rebar identity create` is idempotent (no-op if identity already exists) or must be guarded with a pre-check, and add an explicit note on expected behavior if run a second time.
findings.finding: The idempotency of `rebar identity create` under re-execution is unspecified. TOFU (Trust On First Use) semantics suggest a second invocation for the same email could either error or silently overwrite the registered public key, making the bootstrap step non-idempotent and potentially unsafe under retry.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-check step: 'Run `rebar list --type identity | grep joeoakhart+bot` first; skip creation if the identity already exists.' Or confirm that `rebar identity create` is idempotent and state so explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the idempotency behaviour of `rebar identity create` (does it error on duplicate, upsert, or create a second entry?) and add a recovery/rotation step to the plan.
findings.finding: The plan does not address idempotency of the `rebar identity create` command: if the command is re-run (e.g., after a partial failure or key rotation), it is unspecified whether it is safe, errors out gracefully, or creates a duplicate identity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Record the per-environment SSM path convention (e.g. `/rebar/{env}/rebar-bot-signing-key`) and explicitly state whether non-prod environments use a separate key or are intentionally unsigned, with a rationale.
findings.finding: Only a prod SSM path is named; no equivalent non-prod storage location or separate non-prod bot key is mentioned, leaving it ambiguous whether dev/stage automation shares the prod signing key or has no signing key at all.
findings.location: Plan body — 'AWS SSM `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`; GitHub Actions secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit recovery/rollback procedure for the case where the private key is lost after `rebar identity create` but before secure storage is confirmed (e.g., how to revoke/re-register the identity, or a checkpoint that verifies storage before the step is considered complete).
findings.finding: Key generation followed by TOFU identity registration is a two-step irreversible operation, but the plan specifies no explicit rollback or recovery procedure if the private key is lost between generation and successful storage (e.g., if the process is interrupted after `rebar identity create` but before writing to SSM/GitHub Secrets).
findings.location: Plan body — 'Decide/record the secure storage locations' step; AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: 'Verify key is durably stored (SSM write confirmed OR local encrypted backup) BEFORE the identity registration is considered complete. If storage fails, document the rebar command to revoke/delete the identity ticket so the bootstrap can be re-run cleanly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add a note in the plan) that `rebar list --type identity` works despite `identity` not being listed in `rebar list --help`, or substitute an alternative verification command (e.g., `rebar show <id>`) in AC1.
findings.finding: The AC verification command `rebar list --type identity` is not documented as valid in the CLI help text (`--type=<type>  Filter by ticket type (bug, epic, story, task)`). While the underlying filter code is generic and accepts any ticket_type value, an operator following the help text would have no indication this command works, and the AC could be misread as relying on undocumented behavior.
findings.location: AC: '`rebar list --type identity` shows it' / src/rebar/_cli/help/list.txt:2 / tests/golden/cli_help/list__help.out:2
findings.suggested_fix: Add `identity` (and `session_log`, `code_review`) to the `--type` help text in `src/rebar/_cli/help/list.txt`, or note in the AC that this relies on the generic type-filter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry capturing the rationale for a shared (vs. per-env) bot identity, the TOFU registration model, gate-exemption for the identity type, and the ed25519 key choice.
findings.finding: The plan establishes a foundational shared bot signing identity used across AWS and GitHub environments — a novel cross-environment identity contract — but no ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc update is called for to capture the architectural rationale (shared vs. per-env identity, TOFU model, gate-exemption for identity type, ed25519 choice).
findings.location: Plan body — 'Bootstrap the shared Rebar Bot signing identity' section; AC item 1 and 3
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNNN-rebar-bot-shared-identity.md` with sections: Context (why shared), Decision (ed25519, TOFU, gate-exempt bootstrap), Consequences (key rotation path, per-env provisioning dependency). Reference it from the plan's 'record' step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE/rotation section that specifies: how to revoke or replace the identity ticket, how to update the AWS SSM and GitHub Actions secret, and what consumers must do during a key rotation.
findings.finding: The plan defines no key rotation, revocation, or decommission path for the shared bot identity and keypair. Once created, there is no documented procedure for retiring or replacing the credential if it is compromised or the bot is decommissioned.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Decide/record the secure storage locations' section; Acceptance Criteria overall
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Key Rotation & Retirement' subsection: (1) rotation procedure — re-generate keypair, run `rebar identity update` (or create + deprecate old), update SSM and GH secret, redeploy consumers; (2) revocation — `rebar identity delete` or equivalent; (3) note which pipeline jobs must be re-triggered after rotation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the consumers of REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY / AWS SSM key, and specify whether each consumer is expected to hard-fail or skip signing when the secret is absent.
findings.finding: Consumers of the shared bot identity are not enumerated, and it is unspecified whether consumers fail-safe or hard-fail when the secret is absent (e.g., before per-env provisioning runs).
findings.location: Plan body — 'Decide/record the secure storage locations' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate existing consumers of `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (CLAUDE.md agent instructions, Gerrit infra scripts, GitHub Actions workflows) and explicitly state which—if any—require updates to wire `rebar identity use <id>` or rely solely on the git-email fallback path.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.md (line 552) and multiple Gerrit/infra scripts already reference `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` as the committer email for `RebarBotNava` — these are live callers of the exact email the new identity ticket will register. The plan does not enumerate or assess whether these callers need updating (e.g., to explicitly use the identity ticket via `rebar identity use <id>`) once the identity exists.
findings.location: Plan body: 'Create the identity: rebar identity create … --email joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com …' / CLAUDE.md line 552 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale sentence explaining why a single shared bot identity (rather than per-environment identities) is the right approach — e.g., unified authorship attribution across environments, simpler TOFU registration, and single key-rotation path.
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale explains only why the write is gate-exempt (unblocked under enforcement), but never states why a single shared identity across both AWS and GitHub environments is the right structural choice (vs. per-environment identities). The 'why it fits' for the design decision is absent.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Create the identity' bullet; 'Decide/record the secure storage locations' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan body: 'A single shared identity is used across both environments because the bot's authorship attribution should be environment-agnostic (one author, one identity in the log), TOFU needs to happen only once, and key rotation is a single operation. Per-environment identities would fragment attribution and require separate bootstrap steps without security benefit at this authority tier.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the IAM least-privilege contract for the SSM parameter (`/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`): name the role(s) permitted to read it, require `ssm:GetParameter` + `kms:Decrypt` scoped to that exact path, and explicitly prohibit wildcard read access — even as a reference constraint for the per-env provisioning tasks.
findings.finding: The plan names `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` as the SSM storage destination for the private key but specifies no IAM policy scoping — no statement of which roles or principals are permitted to read or write this parameter, and no prohibition on wildcard or over-broad access.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Decide/record the secure storage locations (AWS SSM `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or a plan note: 'SSM parameter must be encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key; only the bot execution role ARN (and break-glass admin role) may hold `ssm:GetParameter` + `kms:Decrypt` on `arn:aws:ssm:*:*:parameter/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
